provenance.pin_detail: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

privilege_notes: runs fetched package code at launch

findings.title: Installed on-the-fly via a package runner
findings.detail: Server 'openbrand' is fetched and executed at launch via 'npx' with auto-accept flag(s) -y; package spec: openbrand-mcp.
findings.recommendation: Install the server as a pinned dependency and invoke the local binary, or vendor it. If a runner is unavoidable, pin an exact version (see unpinned_source).

findings.title: Package source is unpinned or mutable
findings.detail: Server 'openbrand' package spec 'openbrand-mcp' is unpinned (pin strength: none): no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now).
findings.recommendation: Pin an exact version (pkg@1.2.3) or a git commit SHA. Avoid @latest and branch refs.

evidence.reason: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

findings.title: Server requires sensitive credentials
findings.detail: Server 'openbrand' requires credential-like env keys: OPENBRAND_API_KEY.
findings.recommendation: Confirm each credential is scoped to least privilege and rotatable. Note which tools can exfiltrate it.

tools.name: extract_brand_assets
tools.description: Extract brand assets (logos, colors, backdrop images, brand name) from a website URL. Returns structured data with logo URLs, hex colors with usage hints, backdrop/OG images, and the detected brand name.

candidate_capabilities.title: Outbound network / data egress
candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that makes outbound calls can exfiltrate anything it can read (including the server's own credentials) to an attacker-chosen host.

toxic_combinations.title: Read-then-send exfiltration chain
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can both access secrets/credentials and make outbound network calls — a path to exfiltrate them to an attacker-chosen host. No tool explicitly reads secrets — this is the server's own required credential plus an egress path (true of most credentialed API servers); confirm egress targets are fixed/trusted before treating as active exfiltration.
